reviews.review_description: While i admit that i liked the overall story and the message given in this 13 minute movie, the image quality was so underwhelming and if the quality was low throughout every part maybe it wouldn't bother me this much but it was inconsistent, some parts were good but some parts were like i had the worst vision ever. There are apps on the market that offer 10 times the quality while taking up a quarter of the space (4.6gb). And, the image becomes scrambled when looking down or moving your head is impossible as it's very disorienting and completely breaks the immersion. I appreciate the effort and would like to see other experiences like this but i would also appreciate some consistency and good quality.b

reviews.review_title: It's pretty cool, but it needs fixes
reviews.review_description:
It's a fantastic concept and the visuals are stunning. The film is only 13 minutes long, but it's perfectly reasonable for the price. 

The downsides include the movement, which is nauseating even if you are used to the VR. And I found that some of the environments were too quick to end as I barely had time to just glance around. I also didn't enjoy that landscapes faded in and out blending with the new landscape. It was nauseating and it detracted from the story. However, those are easy fixes.

Overall, It's a great experience and I did enjoy it
Tried everything this movie/ experience will not work don't know if it's a bug or what. Or the devs gave up on this project. Unplayable. Do not get save your money.

I changed my review from one star to five. Originally, I could not download Genesis. After about the seventh try it started to download. The download is 4.8 Gb (it says 4.7 when the download starts). I liked the experience overall but only after downloading to use it.
The graphics and quality were amazing. It was a very immersive experience and very educational. I want to show it to my son soon.
Coolest 13 minutes of your life but wish it was longer!

This is such a cool experience. This is the sort of thing I love showing new VR users. It is informative, visually appealing, and just plain cool. 

As noted by others, there are a couple of scenes that are a little blurry, which is the only reason that I didn't give this five stars.

Yes, it is on the shorter side, but it still matches the price you pay. Definitely worth it. Just wish it was crystal clear throughout.

Hope more VR documentaries like this come out
Wow wow wow, just a amazing wow

Genesis is a 360-degree stereoscopic VR feature that delves into an epic subject in twelve short minutes. By visualizing the history of the Earth as a single day through a sequence of scenes, Genesis covers everything from the formation of the Moon and the Cambrian Explosion to the evolution of mammals into early humans, leading to the present day. Depicting such an extraordinary scope in such a small runtime can never resolve to anything greater than skipping a stone over the surface of Earth's story, yet Genesis accomplishes its goals through big scenes underlined by a broad narrative.

As a spectacle, the visual depth of several scenes left us impressed, and the suitably bombastic and atmospheric musical score adds an excellent layer of immersion. However, we were largely frustrated by two factors. Firstly, rather than being a structured film with a cohesive style, the production felt more like a montage of individual shots that rarely, if ever, utilizes the unique persectives offered by virtual reality.

Our second frustration stemmed from the fact that, although some scenes were well-rendered and graphically detailed, others looked remarkably blurry, as if we were peering through cataracts. Rendering and displaying stereoscopic 360-degree surround video on a device such as the Meta Quest necessarily requires compromises on graphic fidelity, but this doesn't explain how the quality of the video can vary from one scene to the next. We suspect this is partly due to the choice of scenes - a detailed churning ocean looks incredibly fuzzy, but later a static forest scene appears well rendered.

As all the movement and action in Genesis are framed towards the front of the viewing area, we wonder whether a high resolution 180-degree video might be a better compromise to better represent Genesis' visuals.

✅ Epic subject, told with objectivity.
✅ Impressive visual spectacles and immersive soundtrack.
❌ Mixed video quality detracts from enjoyment.

Really good cinema style vr experience but I was expecting something a bit longer than 13minutes but VR cinema is heading in the right direction if this is what's the come. Some eerie scenes too esp undertone water. CGI is amazing. But for £1.49 I'm glad to support the company in the projects all the same. Thanks.
State of the art VR experience from Germany. I also watched their 1st and 2nd step which is available in AppLab.
Download speed is very slow, online viewing is not good, hope to repair the server. In 2023, at least 8k HD should be done?
